set.body: gale QR at n=256 is 0.23x pure-JVM Breeze (least squares 0.27x) after kernel tuning — the gap is algorithmic: unblocked Householder applies reflectors one at a time (gemv-bound), while LAPACK-lineage QR aggregates them (WY / compact-WY representation) into gemm-shaped updates. Implement blocked Householder QR (panel factorization + trailing-matrix update via the tuned gemm/gemmPanel), preserving the compact reflectors+tau storage contract and lazy q (handoff invariant). Re-measure QR + least-squares + downstream (Lstsq drives the gap). Baselines: benchmarks/results/2026-07-10-breeze-{baseline,tuned}.md. Also re-measure Solve/Chol at 256 on a quiet machine first — their tuned-sweep dips may be background-load noise.
